title: How Bad Colds and COVID Can Affect Lungs Later
summary: This article talks about how getting very sick with COVID-19 or the flu might make it easier for lung cancer to grow later on. Scientists from UVA Health found that when people have severe lung infections, it can change the cells in their lungs in a way that helps cancer develop faster. They studied mice and people and saw that those with bad infections were more likely to get lung cancer years later. The good news is that getting vaccinated can help prevent these harmful changes. Doctors now suggest watching patients who recover from severe COVID or flu closely so they can catch any cancer early when it's easier to treat. This research helps us understand why taking care of our lungs is important, especially after a serious illness.

What is the main message about vaccination in the article?

background_read: Lung cancer is a serious disease where bad cells grow in the lungs, making it hard to breathe. COVID-19 and the flu are illnesses caused by viruses that can infect the lungs. When people get very sick from these, it can damage their lungs. Scientists study how this damage might lead to problems like cancer later on. Vaccines are shots that help your body fight off viruses better, so you don't get as sick. This article explains new research showing that severe infections can change lung cells in a way that helps cancer, but vaccines can protect against this.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists from UVA Health and patients who had severe COVID-19 or flu
WHAT: Found that severe lung infections can make it easier for lung cancer to develop later
WHERE: In the lungs of mice and humans studied in research labs
WHY: To understand how infections affect cancer risk and how vaccines can help prevent it

perspectives.description: Doctors should monitor patients after severe infections to catch cancer early.
